executiveAssessment.executiveNarrative: Multiple mechanisms appear to be manifestations of a deeper dependency on centralized approval, unclear authority, or slow decision escalation. This theory is supported by Governance Friction, Decision Latency, Accountability Gap, Coordination Breakdown. Discovery formed this as a higher-order organizational theory because several signals suggest decision flow is constrained by authority, escalation, or approval structure. It interprets the persistent semantic cohort "Project Pattern may be shaping the strategic picture." and is supported by 8 semantic observations, 3 cognitive layers, 3 matched canonical concepts, 8 occurrences. This candidate classifies canonical semantic memory rather than rebuilding semantic meaning from raw observations. Discovery formed this as a higher-order organizational theory because several signals suggest decision flow is constrained by authority, escalation, or approval structure. It interprets the persistent semantic cohort "Leadership confidence may be outpacing the evidence." and is supported by 16 semantic observations, 6 cognitive layers, 4 mechanism-level supports, 7 matched canonical concepts, 16 occurrences. This candidate classifies canonical semantic memory rather than rebuilding semantic meaning from raw observations. Discovery selected Centralized governance bottleneck is constraining the organization because it has the strongest combined confidence, coverage, stability, and explanatory power among available organizational theories. It is supported by 4 mechanism(s) and 1 organizational belief(s). Discovery assigns 89% confidence because the dominant theory has support from 4 mechanism(s) and 1 organizational belief(s). Confidence is not treated as certainty because Organizational continuity failure is emerging remains partially plausible at approximately 87% theoretical strength. Confidence is reduced by 4 weak, competing, or contradictory signal(s). Treat Centralized governance bottleneck is constraining the organization as the current working theory. Focus intervention on Governance Friction, Decision Latency, Coordination Breakdown, while collecting evidence that could confirm, weaken, or falsify this explanation.
